subject: Sociology
title: Émile Durkheim and Social Facts
description: Learn Émile Durkheim and Social Facts in one minute.

hook.text: Suicide seems like the most personal act imaginable. Durkheim proved it follows predictable social patterns.

buildup.text: Durkheim argued that social facts — norms, institutions, collective beliefs — exist outside any individual.

discovery.text: He showed that suicide rates varied by religion, marital status, and social integration, not just psychology.

twist.text: This was radical: society isn't just individuals added together. It has its own forces that constrain behavior.

climax.text: Durkheim established sociology as a distinct science by proving society shapes us more than we shape it.

punchline.text: Even your most private choices carry society's fingerprint.

quiz.question: What did Durkheim demonstrate about suicide?
options: It is purely a psychological phenomenon
